# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) 

# Bind to all interfaces only accessible via HTTP with a given IP address if behind a Reverse Proxy, such as Nginx or Apache in AWS environment; default False    
http.port: "9200" # Default is not exposed within cluster (security concerns), usually set according your application logic 51678 for production environments only  
network.host: "_eth0_"   	# Bind to all interfaces on the loopback device in order of preference, and default bind address otherwise if none specified by command line option --bind_address     	     network.host : "localhost" # Defaults is not exposed within cluster (security concerns), usually set according your application logic 
http.cors:   	# Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications, defaults to ["*"]. For a security reason you should probably be more strict with this    	   elasticsearch.cluster_name : "myCluster" # Cluster name is not accessible within ElasticSearch cluster due permission restrictions 
node.store: ".data/_node/"	# where the node data will store by default, it's often located under a specific location in your file system (such as /var/lib/elasticsearch/)  	       http.enable_cors : "all" # Enable CORS for all origins 
cluster.name: "myCluster0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"	# Set a specific name if you want to have multiple clusters in your environment 
network.host: "localhost"# Bind address for Elasticsearch server, default binds all interfaces  	     discovery.seed_hosts : ["http://127.0.0.1:9356"]	# Set the initial set of master nodes to discover when starting up in a multi-master setup 
discovery.type: "single-node" # Type if you're using Elasticsearch as your single node discovery mechanism, defaults to ["single_node"]. Multi Master should be used for more complex topology configurations (e.g., replicas & shards)    cluster.initial_master_nodes : []
